arts.ttl: White and Timber start, Saka partners Merino - Arsenal line-ups vs Real Madrid
arts.sum: Canton’s Arsenal XI vs Real Madrid – Raya; Timber, White, Saliba, Lewis-Skelly; Partey, Rice, Odegaard; Saka, Merino, Martinelli Arsenal take on Real Madrid in the UEFA Champions League quarter-final looking to pull off one of the biggest wins in the Emirates Stadium era.

But we head into the first leg knowing that no team has beaten us in north London in 10 Champions League games since our return last term, and an extension of that run would put us in a superb place before our visit to the Spanish capital eight days later. Los Blancos will make their first-ever competitive appearance at Emirates Stadium, but while the 15-time winners have beaten 111 teams in the competition’s history, we are not one of them courtesy of our famous last-16 success in the only previous meaningful meetings between the teams.

arts.ttl: Bukayo Saka confirms Arsenal new contract stance and has one demand ahead of Real Madrid
arts.sum: Arsenal winger Bukayo Saka has returned from injury in time for the Gunners' clash against Real Madrid in the Champions League, and wants to win a trophy with the north London side

arts.ttl: Arsenal sent clear Real Madrid instruction to stop Vinicius Jr, Kylian Mbappe and Jude Bellingham
arts.sum: It will be the first time the two sides have met in a competitive fixture since 2006, when Arsenal knocked Madrid out of the Champions League at the round of 16 stage. Emmaneul Eboue has revealed what Arsenal must do to stop the likes of Vinicius Junior, Kylian Mbappe and Jude Bellingham from performing for Real Madrid.
